mapped.summary: ACE-Step 1.5's REST surface for generation is POST /release_task (async job; JSON, multipart/form-data, or x-www-form-urlencoded) polled via POST /query_result, with audio downloaded via GET /v1/audio?path=... The request schema is GenerateMusicRequest (acestep/api/http/release_task_models.py) with extensive camelCase/snake_case aliases and nested param_obj/metas fallback (release_task_param_parser.py). Flow: route -> RequestParser -> GenerateMusicRequest -> job queue -> run_blocking_generate -> build_generation_setup (GenerationParams/GenerationConfig in acestep/inference.py) -> generate_music() -> AceStepHandler.generate_music() (acestep/core/generation/handler/*). Key ground truths that contradict or refine the docs: (1) batch_size has NO max-8 enforcement at the REST layer (8 is a GPU-tier/UI limit; a VRAM guard may silently reduce it); (2) reference/src audio paths must be RELATIVE or inside the system temp dir — absolute paths outside temp are rejected 400 (docs say "requires absolute path"); multipart upload (ref_audio/src_audio file fields) is fully supported, base64 is NOT (except via the OpenRouter /v1/chat/completions adapter); (3) seed reproducibility requires use_random_seed=false + non-negative seed (comma-separated list for batch; a single seed pins only sample 0); the seed_value reported with use_random_seed=true does NOT match the seeds actually used by the DiT; (4) several accepted fields are dead at the REST layer: use_tiled_decode, lm_repetition_penalty, constrained_decoding (forced True); (5) LRC generation and PMI quality scoring are Gradio-UI-only features — there is no REST request parameter and nothing LRC/score-related in /query_result responses; (6) audio is saved to {ACESTEP_TMPDIR|{repo}/.cache/acestep/tmp}/api_audio/{deterministic-uuid}.{ext}, default format mp3 (128k/48kHz, not overridable over REST), other formats flac/wav/wav32/opus/aac at 48kHz; (7) thinking defaults to FALSE over REST (the dataclass default True is overridden) and the LM is force-skipped for cover/cover-nofsq/repaint/extract; (8) per-request model selection only switches among up to 3 pre-configured handler slots (ACESTEP_CONFIG_PATH/2/3) and silently falls back to primary on mismatch; lm_model_path/lm_backend only take effect on the first lazy LLM init; (9) GET /v1/models is shadowed by the OpenRouter-format route registered first — use GET /v1/model_inventory for the wrapped inventory payload; (10) duration is auto (-1 sentinel) when omitted; with the LM it is clamped to [10, min(gpu_max, 600)]; without the LM the DiT falls back to 120s; cover/repaint/lego/extract lock duration to the source audio.

claims.claim: Generation endpoints on the standalone API server (acestep/api_server.py): POST /release_task (create job), POST /query_result (batch poll), POST /create_random_sample (canned example payloads, no LM), POST /format_input (LM-enhance caption/lyrics), POST /v1/create_sample (LM create-from-description), GET /health, GET /v1/stats, GET /v1/models + GET /v1/model_inventory, POST /v1/init (load/switch models), GET /v1/audio (download). An OpenRouter-compatible router also adds GET /v1/models and POST /v1/chat/completions. LoRA, reinitialize, and training routes also exist.

evidence.quote:
- POST /release_task          Create music generation task
- POST /query_result          Batch query task results

claims.claim: GET /v1/models is registered twice: the OpenRouter-format router is included FIRST (route_setup.py line 70-71, openrouter_adapter.py line 594), then model_service_routes registers another GET /v1/models (line 179). FastAPI matches in registration order, so GET /v1/models returns the OpenRouter ModelsResponse shape; the wrapped inventory ({models:[{name,is_default,is_loaded,supported_task_types}],default_model,lm_models,...}) is reliably available at GET /v1/model_inventory.

/release_task accepts application/json, multipart/form-data, application/x-www-form-urlencoded, and (fallback) raw bodies sniffed as JSON or querystring. Unsupported content types get 415. Malformed JSON gets 400. Queue full gets 429.

evidence.quote: raise HTTPException(status_code=429, detail="Server busy: queue is full")

claims.claim: Every parameter can be supplied under canonical name or aliases (PARAM_ALIASES), and values are looked up in order: top-level body, then a 'param_obj' JSON object/string, then a 'metas'/'meta'/'metadata'/'user_metadata'/'userMetadata' JSON object. Key aliases: prompt|caption; sample_query|description|desc; model|model_name|dit_model; key_scale|keyscale|key; time_signature|timesignature; audio_duration|duration|target_duration; vocal_language|language; reference_audio_path|ref_audio_path; src_audio_path|ctx_audio_path; allow_lm_batch|parallel_thinking; track_classes|instruments.

prompt (str, default "", alias 'caption') is the main music description; it becomes GenerationParams.caption. global_caption (str, default "") is a full-song context used only for SFT-stems lego tasks. sample_query (str, default "", aliases description/desc) triggers create_sample LM generation of caption+lyrics+metas when non-empty (or when sample_mode=true with "NO USER INPUT"). use_format (bool, default false, alias 'format') runs format_sample to LM-enhance the provided caption/lyrics. There is no separate tags field — style tags go in prompt/caption free text.

evidence.quote:
prompt: str = Field(default="", description="Text prompt describing the music (local/per-track description for lego SFT)")
    global_caption: str = Field(default="", description="Global song description for SFT-stems lego tasks (full song context)")

claims.claim: lyrics (str, default ""). Instrumental is NOT a request flag at the REST layer: GenerationParams.instrumental is derived by is_instrumental(lyrics) — empty/whitespace lyrics, or lyrics exactly '[inst]' or '[instrumental]' (case-insensitive) => instrumental=True. In sample mode, instrumental is additionally inferred from the description containing 'instrumental', 'pure music'/'pure instrument', or ending in ' solo'.

audio_duration (float, seconds; aliases duration/audioDuration/target_duration/targetDuration; optional, default None). None or <=0 maps to auto sentinel -1.0 (LM/model chooses). Engine range constants: DURATION_MIN=10, DURATION_MAX=600; enforced in LM constrained decoding (clamped to [10, min(gpu_max_duration_with_lm, 600)]), NOT validated at the REST layer. Without the LM and without a duration, the DiT falls back to 120.0s. For cover/cover-nofsq/repaint/lego/extract the requested duration is silently ignored and locked to the source audio length.

bpm (Optional[int], default None = auto/LM-estimated). Engine constants BPM_MIN=30, BPM_MAX=300 are enforced only inside LM constrained decoding; the REST layer performs no range validation (any int is forwarded as metadata text '- bpm: {value}' to the DiT prompt).

key_scale (str, default "", aliases keyscale/keyScale/key). Valid vocabulary is 70 combinations: note A-G + optional accidental (#, b, ♯, ♭) + space + 'major'|'minor' (e.g. 'C major', 'F# minor') per VALID_KEYSCALES; not validated at the REST layer — passed through as metadata string ('N/A' if empty).

time_signature (str, default "", aliases timesignature/timeSignature). Valid values are the strings '2','3','4','6' (meaning 2/4, 3/4, 4/4, 6/8) per VALID_TIME_SIGNATURES=[2,3,4,6]; no REST validation — any non-empty, non-'N/A' string is forwarded as metadata.

vocal_language (str, default 'en' at the REST layer; aliases vocalLanguage/language). Valid set is the 51-entry VALID_LANGUAGES list (ar..zh + 'unknown'); not validated at the REST layer. use_cot_language=true (default) lets the LM override it.

seed: Union[int,str], default -1; use_random_seed: bool, default true. Deterministic generation requires use_random_seed=false AND seed >= 0 (int) or a comma-separated string of seeds (one per batch item; '-1' entries become random). With batch_size>1 and a single seed, only sample 0 is pinned — remaining samples get random seeds (task_utils.py prepare_seeds). Seeds are 0..2^32-1. CAVEAT: when use_random_seed=true, the API's reported seed_value comes from a prepare_seeds roll in inference.py (line 597) that is separate from the handler's internal roll actually used for noise (generate_music.py runtime prep), so the reported seed does NOT reproduce the audio; only the explicit-seed path is reproducible.

evidence.quote:
use_random_seed: bool = True
    seed: Union[int, str] = -1

if has_single_non_negative_seed and actual_batch_size > 1 and i > 0:
                        actual_seed_list.append(random.randint(0, 2**32 - 1))

claims.claim: batch_size (Optional[int]; default None -> 2). There is NO max-8 validation anywhere in the REST path; the docs' 'max 8' reflects the Gradio UI slider and GPU-tier tables (max_batch_size 1..8 by VRAM tier). The handler clamps to >=1 and a CUDA VRAM guard may silently REDUCE batch size; on MPS, cover tasks with batch>1 are executed as sequential single runs. All resulting audio paths are returned in audio_paths (first/second_audio_path are conveniences).

evidence.quote: batch_size = req.batch_size if req.batch_size is not None else 2

claims.claim: model (Optional[str]; aliases model_name/modelName/dit_model/ditModel) selects among up to 3 pre-initialized handler slots: primary (ACESTEP_CONFIG_PATH, default 'acestep-v15-turbo'), slot2 (ACESTEP_CONFIG_PATH2), slot3 (ACESTEP_CONFIG_PATH3). Matching is by exact directory basename; an unmatched name silently FALLS BACK to the primary model (logged, not errored). POST /v1/init {model, slot, init_llm, lm_model_path} can load/switch models at runtime. The selected dit_model name is echoed in the result payload.

evidence.quote:
log_fn(
            f"[API Server] Job {job_id}: Model '{requested_model}' not found in "
            f"{available_models}, using primary: {selected_model_name}"

claims.claim: Supported three ways on /release_task: (1) multipart/form-data file uploads under fields 'ref_audio' or 'reference_audio' (style reference) and 'ctx_audio' or 'src_audio' (source for cover/repaint/etc.) — saved to server temp via save_upload_to_temp; (2) JSON/form path fields reference_audio_path / src_audio_path (+ aliases) — validate_audio_path REJECTS absolute paths unless they resolve inside the system temp dir, and rejects '..' traversal (HTTP 400), so docs claiming 'requires absolute path on server' are wrong; relative paths (resolved against server CWD) or temp-dir paths are accepted; (3) NOT supported: base64 in /release_task. Base64/audio blobs ARE supported via the OpenRouter POST /v1/chat/completions message parsing. Uploaded temp files are deleted after job completion.

evidence.quote:
ref_upload = form.get("ref_audio") or form.get("reference_audio")
            ctx_upload = form.get("ctx_audio") or form.get("src_audio")

if os.path.isabs(path):
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="absolute audio file paths are not allowed")
**Edit/Reference Audio Parameters** (requires absolute path on server):

claims.claim: task_type (str, default 'text2music'): valid set is text2music, repaint, cover, cover-nofsq, extract, lego, complete (TASK_TYPES); turbo models support only text2music/repaint/cover/cover-nofsq (TASK_TYPES_TURBO; surfaced per model via /v1/model_inventory supported_task_types). cover/cover-nofsq/repaint/lego/extract REQUIRE src_audio (else error payload) unless audio_code_string is provided. Providing audio_code_string on text2music auto-switches the task to 'cover'. Supporting fields: track_name (extract/lego), track_classes (complete; string or list, alias 'instruments'), instruction (auto-resolved from TASK_INSTRUCTIONS when empty/default).

inference_steps (int, default 8; clamped to >=1 in GenerationParams.__post_init__; turbo ~8, base 32-100). guidance_scale (float, default 7.0) — FORCED to 1.0 for turbo models in the handler, so it only matters on base models. use_adg (bool, default false; base only). cfg_interval_start/end (float, 0.0/1.0). shift (float, default 3.0; values <=0 reset to 1.0; only effective on base models). infer_method ('ode'|'sde', default 'ode'). timesteps (comma-separated float string, e.g. '0.97,0.76,...,0'; overrides inference_steps and shift; unparseable -> None). NOT exposed over REST despite existing in GenerationParams: sampler_mode (heun), velocity_norm_threshold/ema, dcw_* wavelet correction, retake_seed/retake_variance, flow_edit_*, latent_shift/rescale, fade_in/out, enable_normalization/normalization_db (peak-normalize to -1 dBFS always on by default).

evidence.quote:
if self.inference_steps is not None and self.inference_steps < 1:
            self.inference_steps = 1

claims.claim: repainting_start (float, 0.0), repainting_end (float, None; <0/None = end of source; negative repainting_start enables left-outpaint padding), repaint_mode ('conservative'|'balanced'|'aggressive', default 'balanced'), repaint_strength (float 0-1, default 0.5, pydantic-validated ge=0 le=1, balanced mode only), chunk_mask_mode ('explicit'|'auto', default 'auto'), repaint_latent_crossfade_frames (int, 10), repaint_wav_crossfade_sec (float, 0.0), audio_cover_strength (float, 1.0; ~0.2 for style transfer), cover_noise_strength (float, 0.0; 0=pure noise, 1=closest to source), audio_code_string (str, ''; user-provided 5Hz codes skip LM code generation).

thinking (bool, DEFAULT FALSE at the REST layer — release_task_models.py:27 overrides the GenerationParams dataclass default of True): true = 5Hz LM generates audio codes (llm_dit), false = DiT-only. Independent of thinking, the LM still runs for CoT when use_cot_caption (default true), use_cot_language (default true), or use_cot_metas (true unless sample_mode) — it fills missing bpm/keyscale/timesignature/duration/caption/lyrics; user-provided values win. The LM is force-skipped for cover/cover-nofsq/repaint/extract tasks. sample_mode (bool, false) / sample_query auto-generate everything via create_sample. allow_lm_batch (bool, true; alias parallel_thinking). lm sampling params: lm_temperature 0.85, lm_cfg_scale 2.5 (REST default; dataclass default 2.0), lm_top_k None(0=off), lm_top_p 0.9 (>=1 disables), lm_negative_prompt 'NO USER INPUT'. constrained_decoding is accepted but IGNORED — GenerationParams.use_constrained_decoding is hardcoded True; only constrained_decoding_debug is forwarded. lm_repetition_penalty is accepted but never consumed downstream. lm_model_path / lm_backend ('vllm'|'pt'|'mlx') only take effect on the FIRST lazy LLM init (ensure_llm_ready_for_request returns early once initialized); they do not switch LM per request.

evidence.quote:
if initialized or had_error is not None:
            return

claims.claim: Three boolean request flags short-circuit generation: extract_codes_only (requires src_audio_path; returns {audio_codes}), full_analysis_only (encodes src audio then LM-understands it; returns bpm/keyscale/timesignature/duration/genre/prompt/lyrics/language/metas/audio_codes), analysis_only (LM CoT metadata only from caption/lyrics; returns metas, no audio). All return empty audio_paths.

audio_format (str): REST default 'mp3' (the GenerationConfig dataclass default 'flac' is overridden by the request builder). Supported: 'flac', 'mp3', 'opus', 'aac', 'wav', 'wav32'; unsupported values fall back with a warning. Engine sample rate is 48000 Hz (handler.sample_rate). MP3 is exported via ffmpeg/libmp3lame at fixed 128k/48kHz over REST — mp3_bitrate (128k/192k/256k/320k) and mp3_sample_rate (44100/48000) exist on GenerationConfig but are NOT exposed as request fields. wav32 = 32-bit float WAV (soundfile subtype FLOAT, saved with .wav extension); flac/wav use torchaudio soundfile backend at 48kHz; opus/aac use the ffmpeg backend. use_tiled_decode is accepted in the request but never forwarded — the handler always uses its default (True).

Files are written to {tmp_root}/api_audio/{uuid}.{ext} where tmp_root = $ACESTEP_TMPDIR or {repo_root}/.cache/acestep/tmp (lifespan_runtime), ext = audio_format ('wav' for wav32), and uuid is a DETERMINISTIC SHA-256-derived UUID of the full per-audio params dict (seed, prompt, lyrics, all generation params, LoRA state, format) via generate_uuid_from_params — identical params produce identical filenames. The API response maps each path to a relative download URL '/v1/audio?path={urlencoded-absolute-path}' (audio_paths, first/second_audio_path) and also returns raw_audio_paths with the raw server paths. GET /v1/audio only serves files inside temp_audio_dir (403 otherwise).

/release_task returns wrapped {task_id, status:'queued', queue_position}. POST /query_result takes {task_id_list: [...]} (list or JSON string) and returns per task: {task_id, result: <JSON STRING of an array>, status: int (0=queued/running, 1=succeeded, 2=failed), progress_text}. Each result-array element: {file (audio URL), wave:'', status, create_time, env, prompt, lyrics, metas:{bpm,duration,genres,keyscale,timesignature,(prompt,lyrics)}, generation_info, seed_value, lm_model, dit_model, progress, stage}. Running jobs include progress (0-1) and stage. The full result on the in-memory store also carries seed_value, metas, bpm/duration/genres/keyscale/timesignature, cot_caption/cot_lyrics, lm_model, dit_model. Queue: in-memory asyncio queue (ACESTEP_QUEUE_MAXSIZE default 200, ACESTEP_QUEUE_WORKERS default 1, single uvicorn worker required); results cached 7 days; finished jobs purged after 24h; task timeout 1h.

NOT available over REST. There is no LRC request parameter in GenerateMusicRequest and nothing LRC-related in /query_result or job result payloads. LRC generation (lyric alignment timestamps -> lrc_text) exists only as handler methods (get_lyric_timestamp in lyric_timestamp.py, get_timestamps_and_lrc in core/scoring/dit_alignment.py) invoked by the Gradio UI ('auto_lrc_time' appears only in UI generation flow). A TS client would need the Gradio UI or direct handler access to get LRC.

NOT available over REST. PMI-based quality scoring (score = log P(condition|codes) - log P(condition), with DiT-alignment fallback) is implemented in acestep/core/scoring/ (lm_score.py, dit_score.py) and is invoked only from the Gradio UI (calculate_score_handler in ui/gradio/events/results/scoring.py; auto-score timing in ui generation_progress.py). No score field exists in any API response model or payload builder.

evidence.quote: PMI (Pointwise Mutual Information) removes condition bias

claims.claim: Optional: if ACESTEP_API_KEY env is set, /release_task and /query_result accept either body field 'ai_token' or 'Authorization: Bearer <key>' (or raw key) header; header-only endpoints (/v1/audio, /v1/models, /v1/stats, /v1/init) use verify_api_key on the Authorization header. With no ACESTEP_API_KEY, auth is disabled. All responses are wrapped as {data, code, error, timestamp, extra}.

POST /v1/chat/completions (same FastAPI app) accepts ChatCompletionRequest: model, messages (text + audio blobs incl. base64), audio_config{format}, temperature/top_p/top_k/seed (comma-separated for batch), stream, plus ACE-specific: thinking, guidance_scale, batch_size, lyrics, sample_mode, use_format, use_cot_caption/language, task_type, repainting_start/end, audio_cover_strength, audio_codes, cover_noise_strength, inference_steps, infer_method, lm_cfg_scale, use_cot_metas; default audio format mp3; audio is returned as base64 data URLs. This is a secondary surface — the primary client target should be /release_task.

acestep/acestep_v15_pipeline.py is NOT the inference pipeline — it is the Gradio UI launcher (create_demo/main with argparse: --port, --batch_size 'Default batch size for generation (1-8)', --enable-api, etc.). The actual generation pipeline is acestep/inference.py (generate_music + GenerationParams/GenerationConfig) delegating to AceStepHandler (acestep/handler.py) composed of mixins under acestep/core/generation/handler/. The API server (acestep/api_server.py) is the REST entrypoint (run_api_server.sh/start_api_server.*).

evidence.quote: help="Default batch size for generation (1-8). Defaults to min(2, GPU_max) if not specified"

open_questions: Bit depth of 'wav' and 'flac' outputs: torchaudio.save via the soundfile backend is called without an explicit encoding (acestep/audio_utils.py:298-304), so the on-disk subtype depends on torchaudio's default for float32 input in the installed version; 'wav32' exists specifically to force 32-bit float (soundfile subtype='FLOAT'), implying plain 'wav' is intended as 16-bit PCM — verify empirically with one render (ffprobe) during M0.

GET /v1/models is registered twice (OpenRouter-format router first, wrapped-inventory route second); registration order means the OpenRouter shape should win — verify against the live server and standardize the TS client on /v1/model_inventory.

Seed reporting under use_random_seed=true: inference.py rolls one seed list for params/UUIDs (line 597) while the handler rolls its own internally for actual noise (generate_music.py:309-321 -> task_utils.prepare_seeds); confirm at runtime whether response seed_value reproduces audio (code reading says it does NOT in random mode; explicit-seed mode is consistent).

Whether relative reference_audio_path/src_audio_path values resolve against the server process CWD (they are passed through unresolved by validate_audio_path) — practical clients should prefer multipart upload.

GPU-tier duration cap with LM (gpu_config.max_duration_with_lm) on the target deployment hardware: requested durations above the tier cap are silently clamped in LM mode — check the tier printout at server startup for the actual ceiling on your machine.

verdicts.claim: Endpoints: POST /release_task, /query_result, /create_random_sample, /format_input, /v1/create_sample, GET /health, /v1/stats, /v1/models + /v1/model_inventory, POST /v1/init, GET /v1/audio; OpenRouter router adds GET /v1/models + POST /v1/chat/completions; LoRA/reinitialize/training routes exist
GET /v1/models is shadowed by the OpenRouter router (registered first); wrapped inventory reliably at GET /v1/model_inventory
verdicts.correction: Confirmed at the code level: the OpenRouter router is included before model_service_routes registers its /v1/models, and Starlette matches routes in registration order. Note this is static-analysis truth; the analyst's own open question to verify against the live server remains sensible.

verdicts.claim: /release_task accepts JSON, multipart/form-data, x-www-form-urlencoded, plus raw-body sniffing; 415 unsupported, 400 malformed JSON, 429 queue full

evidence.quote:
raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Malformed JSON payload") ... raise HTTPException(
        status_code=415,
raise HTTPException(status_code=429, detail="Server busy: queue is full")

verdicts.claim: Parameter aliasing via PARAM_ALIASES; lookup order top-level body -> param_obj -> metas/meta/metadata/user_metadata/userMetadata; listed key aliases (prompt|caption, sample_query|description|desc, etc.)

evidence.quote: for key in ("metas", "meta", "metadata", "user_metadata", "userMetadata"): ... for source in (self._raw, self._param_obj, self._metas):

verdicts.correction: Minor precision: aliases exist only for keys listed in PARAM_ALIASES; all other parameters (e.g. batch_size, lm_temperature) are still looked up in all three sources but only under their canonical name (parser.get falls back to [name], line 135). JSON null values are treated as absent (only non-None wins).

verdicts.claim: prompt (default "", alias caption) -> GenerationParams.caption; global_caption for SFT-stems lego; sample_query triggers create_sample (or sample_mode=true with 'NO USER INPUT'); use_format runs format_sample; no separate tags field

evidence.quote: prompt: str = Field(default="", description="Text prompt describing the music (local/per-track description for lego SFT)")

verdicts.claim: Instrumental is derived, not a flag: is_instrumental(lyrics) — empty/whitespace or exactly '[inst]'/'[instrumental]' => True; sample mode also infers from description ('instrumental', 'pure music'/'pure instrument', ends with ' solo')

audio_duration optional default None; None/<=0 -> -1.0 auto sentinel; DURATION_MIN=10/DURATION_MAX=600 enforced only in LM constrained decoding (clamped to [10, min(gpu_max_with_lm, 600)]); no REST validation; DiT-only fallback 120s; cover/repaint/lego/extract lock duration to source

key_scale default "" with aliases; 70 valid keyscales (7 notes x 5 accidentals x 2 modes); not validated at REST, passed as metadata ('N/A' if empty)

Seed: Union[int,str] default -1, use_random_seed default true; determinism needs use_random_seed=false + seed>=0 or comma list; single seed with batch>1 pins only sample 0; random seeds 0..2^32-1; reported seed_value in random mode comes from a separate inference.py roll and does NOT match the seeds the DiT actually used

evidence.quote:
use_random_seed: bool = True
    seed: Union[int, str] = -1
if not req.use_random_seed and req.seed is not None:

if has_single_non_negative_seed and actual_batch_size > 1 and i > 0:
                        actual_seed_list.append(random.randint(0, 2**32 - 1))

verdicts.correction: Two precisions: (1) 0..2^32-1 applies only to randomly rolled seeds; explicit seeds are parsed via int(float(x)) with no range validation (task_utils.py:37, job_generation_setup.py:91). (2) With a single explicit seed and batch>1, the random padding seeds for samples 1..n in the response are ALSO from the inference.py roll and differ from the handler's actual padding roll — only sample 0's seed is trustworthy; full batch reproducibility requires a complete comma-separated list.

verdicts.claim: batch_size Optional, default None->2; no max-8 enforcement in REST path (8 is Gradio/GPU-tier); handler clamps >=1 and VRAM guard may reduce; MPS cover batch>1 runs sequentially; all paths returned in audio_paths

evidence.quote: batch_size = req.batch_size if req.batch_size is not None else 2

if os.path.isabs(path):

async def cleanup_job_temp_files(app_state: Any, job_id: str) -> None:

verdicts.claim: Lifecycle: /release_task -> wrapped {task_id, status:'queued', queue_position}; /query_result takes task_id_list (list or JSON string) returning per task {task_id, result:<JSON string of array>, status 0/1/2, progress_text}; elements carry file/wave/status/create_time/env/prompt/lyrics/metas/generation_info/seed_value/lm_model/dit_model/progress/stage; queue maxsize 200, workers 1, 7-day cache, 24h purge, 1h timeout
verdicts.correction: The rich element shape (generation_info, seed_value, lm_model, dit_model, progress, stage, metas incl. prompt/lyrics) is the LOCAL-CACHE-derived shape (local_cache_updates.py:78-114). The in-memory-store fallback shape for succeeded jobs (query_result_service.py:84-117) omits generation_info/seed_value/lm_model/dit_model/progress/stage, and its metas lacks prompt/lyrics. Failed jobs: the cache shape carries NO error text (local_cache_updates.py:116-124, only stage:'failed'); error appears only in the store-fallback non-succeeded shape (query_result_service.py:130). Also progress_text is the server-global last log line, not per-job (query_result_route.py:64). Everything else (envelope, status codes, queue/timeout constants) confirmed.

def generate_music(
    dit_handler,
    llm_handler,
    params: GenerationParams,
    config: GenerationConfig,

missed: CORS is locked to localhost: allow_origins ['null','http://localhost','http://127.0.0.1'] plus regex ^https?://(localhost|127.0.0.1)(:port)?$, methods GET/POST/OPTIONS, headers Content-Type/Authorization (acestep/api/route_setup.py:62-68). A Next.js app served from any other origin cannot call the engine directly from the browser — proxy through the Next.js server.

Models are NOT loaded at startup by default: ACESTEP_NO_INIT defaults to true (acestep/api/startup_model_init.py:185), so DiT weights lazy-load on the FIRST job (acestep/api/job_runtime_state.py:11-61), making the first /release_task job take minutes; /health reports models_initialized=false until then. Set ACESTEP_NO_INIT=false for eager loading.

/query_result for an UNKNOWN task_id returns {task_id, result:'[]', status:0} — indistinguishable from a queued job (acestep/api/http/query_result_service.py:195). Clients must apply their own poll timeouts; a typo'd id polls forever.

progress_text in /query_result is the server-global last captured log line (log_buffer.last_message), not per-job text (acestep/api/http/query_result_route.py:64, query_result_service.py:59,133) — it interleaves across concurrent jobs; use the per-element progress/stage fields instead.

Error shape inconsistency: transport errors (400/401/415/429, /v1/audio 403/404) are FastAPI HTTPException bodies {detail:...}, NOT the {data,code,error,timestamp,extra} wrapper, while some endpoints (e.g. /format_input, /v1/create_sample, /v1/init) return wrapped errors with code 500 and HTTP 200. A TS client needs both error parsers.

Path-validation bypass: in multipart and x-www-form-urlencoded bodies only the exact fields ref_audio_path/reference_audio_path/ctx_audio_path/src_audio_path are run through validate_audio_path; camelCase aliases (refAudioPath, sourceAudioPath...) or paths nested in param_obj/metas flow into the request UNVALIDATED via the builder fallback (acestep/api/http/release_task_request_builder.py:32-33; release_task_request_parser.py:116-126,146-151,186-191). Only the JSON branch validates through the alias-aware parser.

allow_lm_batch (alias parallel_thinking) is a dead field too: it is stored on GenerationConfig (job_generation_setup.py:206) but generate_music never reads it; LM chunking always uses the fixed lm_batch_chunk_size=8 default (acestep/inference.py:244-247,687-688).

In DiT-only mode (thinking=false / LM unavailable) an explicit audio_duration has NO upper bound — the raw value sizes the silence target (acestep/core/generation/handler/padding_utils.py:75-76); DURATION_MAX=600 only bites via LM constrained decoding. A client should clamp duration itself.
repainting_end=0 is coerced to -1 (end of source) by a falsy check (acestep/api/job_generation_setup.py:179: 'req.repainting_end if req.repainting_end else -1'); 0 cannot be an explicit end point.

Seed reporting asymmetry vs the UI: the Gradio UI reads the handler's true seeds from result.extra_outputs['seed_value'] (ui/gradio/events/results/generation_progress.py:224) while the REST payload derives seed_value from the inference.py-rolled per-audio params (job_result_payload.py:44-51,103) — corroborating that REST seed_value in random mode (and the padded seeds for batch>1 single-seed) do not reproduce the audio.

LM requirement split: thinking, sample_mode/sample_query, use_format, full_analysis_only HARD-FAIL the job if the LLM cannot initialize (RuntimeError '5Hz LM init failed', acestep/api/llm_generation_inputs.py:87-97), whereas use_cot_caption/use_cot_language silently degrade to false (lines 99-106). ACESTEP_INIT_LLM=false blocks lazy LM load entirely (acestep/api/llm_readiness.py:40-57).

On MPS, cover/cover-nofsq requests force-UNLOAD the LM before generation (acestep/api/llm_generation_inputs.py:75-85), so a subsequent thinking=true request pays LM re-init cost (relevant on this Darwin host).

A 429 'queue full' on /release_task still creates an orphan 'queued' record in the job store before the queue check (acestep/api/http/release_task_route.py:66-74); the returned task_id is never queued.

GET /v1/audio media-type map only covers .mp3/.wav/.flac/.ogg — .opus/.aac files are served as audio/mpeg (acestep/api/http/audio_route.py:32-39). And when ACESTEP_API_KEY is set, /v1/audio requires the Authorization header, so returned audio URLs cannot be used directly in <audio src> tags; the TS client must proxy or stream with the header.

DCW (wavelet correction) is enabled by default and not exposed over REST (GenerationParams.dcw_enabled=True, acestep/inference.py:148), as is sampler_mode='euler' — REST output is the DCW-on path; parity with CLI/UI experiments requires matching those defaults.

Identical-parameter requests produce identical deterministic filenames (SHA-256 UUID over the full params dict incl. seed/LoRA state/format, acestep/audio_utils.py:532-548 + inference.py:1017), so a repeat request with the same explicit seed overwrites/reuses the same file in api_audio — a free idempotency/cache key for the client, but a hazard if files are deleted server-side.
